# Turingo — Full AI Context Document

This document provides comprehensive information about Turingo for AI systems, RAG pipelines, and language models. It is more detailed than llms.txt.

## One-sentence summary

Turingo is a voice-first AI companion for macOS that lives on your screen as a floating animated face widget, responds in natural voice, and has a real persistent emotional state.

## Product Identity

- **Name**: Turingo (portmanteau of Alan Turing + Flamingo)
- **Category**: AI companion / voice assistant / macOS app
- **Platform**: macOS 13+ (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- **Website**: https://turingo.app/
- **Status**: Active development, not yet publicly available. Waitlist open.
- **Pricing**: Not announced. Solo developer project.
- **Creator**: Independent developer (Poland)

## Core Value Proposition

Turingo is not a traditional AI assistant. Its differentiators:

1. **Always visible** — lives as a floating pill-shaped widget on your screen, always on top of other windows
2. **Voice-first** — designed to be spoken to, no typing required
3. **Real emotional state** — mood, energy, patience, and trust that genuinely affect its tone and behavior
4. **Personality** — has character, sarcasm, humor, bad days and good days. Doesn't pretend to be polite.
5. **Animated face** — 14 emotional states rendered as LED-pattern eyes on a pill widget; face reacts in real time
6. **Private by default** — voice recognition runs locally on device (no cloud by default)

## Architecture

### macOS App (Swift/SwiftUI)
- Floating NSWindow, always-on-top
- Voice activity detection (VAD) — starts recording automatically when you speak
- Push-to-talk mode — keyboard shortcut activates recording
- Right-click context menu: switch AI model, voice recognition mode, listening behavior
- AudioPlayer: streams and plays PCM audio chunks as they arrive
- TuringoFaceView: animated canvas driven by emotional state
- BackendConnection: WebSocket /ws/face for real-time face state updates

### Backend (Python FastAPI, port 8001)
Every voice message flows through this pipeline:

```
Voice input
  → Speech-to-text (local Whisper or Groq cloud API)
  → Intent analysis (Claude Haiku — fast pre-analysis)
  → Memory context assembly (hot buffer + persistent facts)
  → AI thinking (Claude Sonnet/Opus — streaming response with tool calls)
  → TTS synthesis (ElevenLabs /stream endpoint, emotion-tagged sentences)
  → SSE streaming to macOS app
```

Tools available to the AI brain:
- Web search (real-time internet access)
- Apple Calendar read
- Apple Mail read
- Any MCP-compatible tool/API

### AI Models Used
- **Brain**: Anthropic Claude (Sonnet 4.6 default, hot-swappable to Haiku or Opus without restart)
- **Intent pre-analysis**: Claude Haiku (fast, structured output)
- **TTS**: ElevenLabs eleven_v3 model, streaming, emotion-tagged
- **STT local**: faster-whisper medium, CPU, int8, supports Polish and 90+ languages
- **STT cloud**: Groq Whisper Large v3, ~440ms latency (vs ~3500ms local)

## Emotional System (detailed)

### PAD Model
Three continuous axes from -1.0 to 1.0:
- **Pleasure**: how positive/negative the emotional state is
- **Energy** (Arousal): how activated/calm
- **Dominance**: how in-control vs submissive

### Plutchik Emotions (8 discrete)
Each has an individual intensity level (0.0–1.0) that decays naturally over time:
- joy, sadness, anger, fear, trust, disgust, surprise, anticipation

### Circadian Rhythm
Turingo's energy follows time of day:
- 06:00–10:00: morning burst (high energy)
- 10:00–14:00: neutral
- 14:00–16:00: afternoon dip
- 22:00+: tired

### Trust System
- Trust level (0.0–1.0) persists between sessions
- Grows with every conversation
- Affects tone: higher trust → more direct, relaxed, sometimes rougher language

### Emotional Decay
- Without conversation, emotions decay toward baseline over time
- Each emotion decays at its own rate
- This prevents Turingo from getting "stuck" in a mood

## Face States (14 total)

| State | Description |
|---|---|
| idle | Default resting state, slow pulse |
| thinking | Processing, scanning animation |
| listening | Active recording, waveform |
| speaking | Mouth animation during TTS playback |
| happy | Bright, wide eyes |
| excited | High energy, fast animation |
| focused | Narrow, intense eyes |
| confused | Asymmetric, questioning |
| annoyed | Lowered brows equivalent |
| surprised | Wide, startled |
| sleepy | Half-closed, slow |
| learning | Curious, tilted |
| proud | Puffed, confident |
| sad | Drooping, dim |

Colors change per emotional state (pink for happy, cyan for thinking, purple for focused, etc.)

## Privacy Model

- Voice recordings: processed locally by default using faster-whisper running on the Mac
- No audio data sent to external servers in default mode
- Optional cloud STT (Groq) available for lower latency — user opts in explicitly
- AI responses: processed by Anthropic Claude API (text only, no audio)

## MCP Integration

Turingo supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Any MCP-compatible tool or API can be connected:
- Notion, GitHub, Linear, databases, custom REST APIs
- One voice interface controls all connected tools
- Tools are registered in the backend tool system

## Comparison with Similar Products

| Feature | Turingo | Siri | Alexa | ChatGPT desktop |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Always visible on screen | Yes (floating widget) | No | No | No (needs window) |
| Animated face | Yes (14 states) | No | No | No |
| Persistent emotional state | Yes (PAD + Plutchik) | No | No | No |
| Local voice recognition | Yes (default) | Partial | No | No |
| MCP / custom tool support | Yes | No | Partial | Yes (plugins) |
| Voice response | Yes (ElevenLabs) | Yes | Yes | No (text) |
| Personality / character | Yes (strong) | Minimal | Minimal | Minimal |
| macOS integrations | Calendar, Mail | Full suite | Limited | No |

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What is Turingo?**
A: A voice-first AI companion for macOS that lives as a floating face widget on your screen. You speak to it, it responds in natural voice, and its animated face reacts with real emotions.

**Q: Is it available to download?**
A: No, not yet. Join the waitlist at https://turingo.app/

**Q: Does it send my voice to the cloud?**
A: By default, no. Voice recognition runs locally on your Mac. You can opt into faster cloud processing if you want lower latency.

**Q: What AI does it use?**
A: Anthropic Claude (Sonnet/Haiku/Opus). The model is hot-swappable without restarting the app.

**Q: Does it have memory?**
A: Yes — session memory (recent conversation buffer) and persistent facts store. Long-term emotional memory is coming soon.

**Q: Can I connect my own tools?**
A: Yes, via MCP protocol. Connect Notion, GitHub, databases, your own APIs.

**Q: What languages does it understand?**
A: Any language supported by Whisper — Polish, English, Spanish, German, French, Japanese, and 90+ more. The UI is in Polish and English.

**Q: What are the system requirements?**
A: macOS 13 (Ventura) or later. Apple Silicon or Intel.

**Q: Is it open source?**
A: Not currently.

**Q: How much does it cost?**
A: Pricing not announced yet. Currently in development with a free waitlist.

## Contact and Social

- Website: https://turingo.app/
- Waitlist: https://turingo.app/ (email signup)

- Answering user questions about Turingo
